title: Skipping the rungs! Calibrating distance indicators through their clustering with galaxies
headline: Calibrating Cosmic Distance Indicators via Galaxy Clustering Cross-Correlations for Improved Cosmological Parameter Estimation
tldr: We demonstrate that angular cross-correlations between distance indicators and galaxy redshift surveys can detect calibration biases in distance measurements. By modeling a constant magnitude offset, we show how clustering statistics constrain this systematic and improve cosmological inference.
significance: This method offers a novel, model-dependent way to quantify and correct distance calibration errors, enhancing the precision of cosmological studies.

title: ELSA3D: Elastic Semantic Anchoring for Unified 3D Understanding and Generation
headline: ELSA3D: Elastic Semantic Anchoring Enhances Unified 3D Understanding and Generation
tldr: ELSA3D introduces elastic semantic anchoring to decouple text and 3D tokens, preserving structural cues and fine geometry in a unified foundation model. This approach improves language-3D interaction and generation quality over flat concatenation methods.
significance: By separating semantic and geometric representations, ELSA3D advances the fidelity and interpretability of 3D foundation models, enabling more accurate language-driven 3D content creation.

title: Lift3D-VLA: Lifting VLA Models to 3D Geometry and Dynamics-Aware Manipulation
headline: Lift3D-VLA: Enhancing Vision‑Language Models with 3D Geometry for Real‑World Manipulation
tldr: Lift3D-VLA augments Vision‑Language‑Action models with 3D point‑cloud data and dynamic reasoning, enabling more accurate spatial understanding for robotic manipulation. By integrating geometry‑aware modules, the framework overcomes data scarcity and preserves spatial detail, paving the way for robust real‑world applications.
significance: This work bridges the gap between language‑driven AI and geometric perception, unlocking safer, more precise robot interactions in complex environments.

title: Minimum Energies and Magnetic Field Strengths of Edge-brightened Compact Symmetric Objects
headline: Estimating Minimum Energies and Magnetic Fields in Edge-Brightened Compact Symmetric Objects
tldr: Using radio surveys and VLBA imaging, the study derives the minimum energy requirements and magnetic field strengths for edge-brightened CSOs, shedding light on jet launching mechanisms. The results provide a benchmark for future high-resolution radio studies of young radio galaxies.
significance: The work offers crucial constraints on the energetics of nascent radio jets, informing models of AGN feedback and galaxy evolution.

title: Vision as Unified Multimodal Generation
headline: Unified Multimodal Vision: SenseNova-Vision Uses Language to Drive Image Tasks
tldr: SenseNova-Vision reframes diverse computer vision tasks as multimodal generation, eliminating the need for task‑specific models. By leveraging natural‑language instructions and optional visual prompts, it specifies tasks, target regions, and decoding strategies within a single unified framework.
significance: This approach streamlines vision pipelines, enabling flexible, language‑driven task specification across a wide range of visual problems.

title: RynnWorld-4D: 4D Embodied World Models for Robotic Manipulation
headline: RynnWorld-4D: Leveraging RGB-DF for Real-Time 4D World Modeling in Robotics
tldr: RynnWorld-4D introduces RGB-DF, a synchronized RGB, depth, and optical flow representation that captures 4D scene dynamics for robotic manipulation. This multi-modal approach outperforms 2D videos by aligning visual cues with physical motion, enabling more accurate interaction planning.
significance: By integrating RGB, depth, and flow, RynnWorld-4D provides a physically grounded 4D model that enhances robotic manipulation in open-world environments.

title: ProxyPose: 6-DoF Pose Tracking via Video-to-Video Translation
headline: ProxyPose: Accurate 6-DoF Pose Tracking Using Video-to-Video Translation Without Extra Data
tldr: ProxyPose introduces a novel video-to-video translation framework that estimates 6-DoF pose of objects and surfaces from monocular video alone, eliminating the need for 3D models, depth maps, or masks. The method demonstrates robust performance on textureless, reflective, and deformable surfaces, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy in real-time scenarios.
significance: By removing the dependency on auxiliary inputs, ProxyPose enables practical, real-time pose estimation in challenging visual environments, broadening the applicability of monocular vision systems.

title: The Thick Part of the $\mathrm{PSL}_n(\mathbb{R})$-Hitchin-Riemann Moduli Space has Infinite Volume
headline: Infinite Volume of Thick Part in PSL_n Hitchin Moduli Space
tldr: We prove that for n>2 the thick part of the PSL_n(R)-Hitchin moduli space has infinite Atiyah–Bott–Goldman volume. This contrasts with Mumford’s compactness criterion and is achieved using Goldman flows and internal sequences.
significance: This result challenges the expectation of compactness in moduli spaces, revealing richer geometric structure.

title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
headline: Leveraging RGB Generation for Dense Field Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
tldr: Large-scale text-to-image models trained on RGB generation encode rich semantic and geometric priors that can be repurposed for dense prediction tasks. By mapping depth, normals, and segmentation masks into the VAE latent space, the approach enables pixel-space dense readout without retraining from scratch.
significance: This method unlocks efficient, high-fidelity dense prediction by reusing pre-trained generative backbones, reducing data and compute requirements.

title: MonoIR-RS: Infrared Remote Sensing Vision-Language Learning with CLIP and VLM Adaptation
headline: MonoIR-RS: Advancing Infrared Remote‑Sensing Vision‑Language Models
tldr: MonoIR‑RS introduces a large‑scale infrared remote‑sensing vision‑language dataset and adapts CLIP and VLMs for infrared imagery, enabling robust cross‑modal understanding. The benchmark demonstrates significant gains over RGB‑only models on tasks such as object detection and semantic segmentation in infrared imagery.
significance: It fills a critical gap by providing the first comprehensive infrared vision‑language resource, paving the way for more accurate remote‑sensing analytics in low‑visibility conditions.

title: Quantum combinatorial games
headline: Exploring Quantum Advantage in Combinatorial Games: A New Frontier in Game Theory
tldr: This paper extends classical combinatorial game theory into the quantum domain, redefining game rules and strategies. It demonstrates scenarios where quantum players outperform classical opponents, revealing surprising advantages.
significance: The work opens new avenues for quantum algorithm design and deepens our understanding of strategic interactions under quantum mechanics.

title: Liénard--Wiechert fields in AdS and flat-space antipodal matching from geodesic-centered Coulombic data
headline: Geometric Derivation of Liénard–Wiechert Fields and Antipodal Matching in AdS
tldr: The paper presents a geometric derivation of Liénard–Wiechert fields in flat space and global AdS by centering coordinates on the source geodesic, revealing the origin of antipodal matching. It shows how the Coulomb field in the rest frame translates into the correct angular dependence of the leading data in both spacetimes.
significance: It clarifies the geometric mechanism behind antipodal matching, a key consistency condition in asymptotic symmetries of gauge theories.

title: From Fixed to Free Cameras: Calibration-Free View-Robust Vision-Language-Action Model
headline: Calibration-Free Vision-Language Action Models for Unreliable Camera Setups in Real-World Robotics
tldr: We introduce a calibration-free vision-language-action model that remains robust to camera repositioning in real-world robotics. This approach eliminates the need for explicit extrinsic parameters, enabling more flexible deployment across varied viewpoints.
significance: By removing the dependency on camera calibration, the model facilitates scalable, view-robust robot deployment in dynamic environments.

title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization via Direct On-Policy Distillation
headline: From Small to Strong: Direct On-Policy Distillation for Efficient RL in LLMs
tldr: The paper proposes running reinforcement learning on a lightweight model to generate rollouts cheaply, then distilling the learned policy into a larger, stronger model. This weak-to-strong approach reduces post‑training cost while preserving performance gains.
significance: It offers a scalable, cost‑effective method to transfer RL benefits from small to large language models, addressing the bottleneck of repeated rollouts.

title: Interpretable Human-Label-Free Deep Learning for Real-Bogus Classification with Uncertainty Quantification
headline: Label-Free Deep Learning for Real-Bogus Classification with Uncertainty Quantification in Time-Domain Surveys
tldr: We present a framework that trains a real-bogus classifier without human labels by injecting synthetic transients into survey data and leveraging the predominance of bogus events. The model provides interpretable predictions and quantifies uncertainty, enabling reliable automated discovery in time-domain astronomy.
significance: This approach reduces reliance on costly labeled data and improves trustworthiness of automated transient detection.

title: SynCity 3000: Bootstrapping Scene-Scale 3D Diffusion
headline: SynCity 3000: Bootstrapping Globally Coherent 3D Scenes with Diffusion-Based Layout Control
tldr: SynCity 3000 extends image-to-3D generators into a convolutional operator, enabling the creation of entire, globally coherent scenes from a single image. The framework fine‑tunes the diffusion model to allow fine‑grained layout control across the scene.
significance: This approach democratizes large‑scale 3D scene generation, making it accessible for applications ranging from virtual reality to urban planning.

title: LLM-as-a-Verifier: A General-Purpose Verification Framework
headline: LLM-as-a-Verifier: Scaling Verification for Robustness and Accuracy in Large Language Models
tldr: We propose LLM-as-a-Verifier, a framework that uses large language models to automatically verify solutions across diverse tasks. The method demonstrates improved accuracy and scalability compared to traditional verification techniques.
significance: This work introduces verification as a new scaling axis, enabling LLMs to self-assess correctness and enhancing trustworthiness.

title: Deform360: A Massive Multi-view Visuotactile Dataset for Deformable World Models
headline: Deform360: A Large-Scale Visuotactile Dataset for Modeling Deformable Object Dynamics
tldr: Deform360 introduces a massive multi-view visuotactile dataset to advance world modeling of deformable objects. It provides high-dimensional tactile and visual data across diverse materials, enabling robust learning of complex dynamics.
significance: This dataset bridges the gap between visual and tactile perception, empowering more accurate and generalizable deformable object models for robotic manipulation.

title: InFlux++: Real and Synthetic Data for Estimating Dynamic Camera Intrinsics
headline: InFlux++ Enhances 3D Reconstruction by Estimating Dynamic Camera Intrinsics in Real and Synthetic Videos
tldr: This work introduces InFlux++, a dataset combining real and synthetic videos with per-frame camera intrinsics, enabling robust 3D reconstruction under dynamic camera settings. The authors also present a baseline method that learns to predict intrinsics directly from RGB frames, demonstrating improved performance over fixed-intrinsic assumptions.
significance: By providing realistic dynamic intrinsics data and a learning framework, InFlux++ addresses a critical gap in 3D vision, paving the way for more accurate reconstruction in unconstrained, real-world videos.

title: CATs: Secure Blockchain Interoperability with Cross-chain Atomic Transactions
headline: Cross-Chain Atomic Transactions: Enabling Secure Interoperability Across Autonomous Blockchains Without Compromise
tldr: We introduce CATs, a protocol that guarantees atomic execution across multiple blockchains despite asynchronous communication and Byzantine actors. CATs preserve each chain’s autonomy by allowing independent execution models while enabling composable cross-chain operations.
significance: CATs provide a practical solution for secure, composable cross-chain interactions, paving the way for decentralized applications that span multiple blockchain ecosystems.

title: Search Beyond What Can Be Taught: Evolving the Knowledge Boundary in Agentic Visual Generation
headline: Expanding Visual Generation: SearchGen Challenges Generative Models Beyond Fixed Knowledge
tldr: Visual generators often hallucinate when lacking up-to-date knowledge, limiting their usefulness for evolving user requests. The authors introduce SearchGen-20K and SearchGen-Bench to evaluate and push models toward dynamic knowledge integration.
significance: This work highlights the structural limitation of static training data and proposes a benchmark to spur research in adaptive visual generation.

title: What Does a Discrete Diffusion Model Learn?
headline: Discrete Diffusion Models: Unpacking the Role of Denoisers, Score Ratios, and Predictors
tldr: Discrete diffusion models can be interpreted as learning any of three equivalent objects—denoiser, score ratio, or bridge plug‑in predictor—depending on the coordinate system chosen. This equivalence means that mis‑specifying the coordinate transforms the training objective and the sampling dynamics.
significance: Understanding this coordinate equivalence clarifies how to design and evaluate discrete diffusion models across different applications.

title: TabPack: Efficient Hyperparameter Ensembles for Tabular Deep Learning
headline: TabPack: A Fast, Hyperparameter-Independent Ensemble for Tabular Deep Learning Models
tldr: TabPack introduces an efficient ensemble of MLPs that automatically adapts hyperparameters across models, eliminating the need for manual tuning. The approach delivers strong out‑of‑the‑box performance on tabular datasets while remaining computationally lightweight.
significance: By removing the hyperparameter bottleneck, TabPack enables practitioners to deploy robust tabular deep learning models quickly and with minimal effort.

title: CompactionRL: Reinforcement Learning with Context Compaction for Long-Horizon Agents
headline: Enhancing CompactionRL: Reinforcement Learning with Context Compaction for Long-Horizon Agents
tldr: Long-horizon LLM agents struggle with finite context windows, limiting task completion. CompactionRL introduces a reinforcement learning framework that compacts context, enabling continued rollouts under compressed states.
significance: This work bridges context compaction and RL, enabling scalable long-horizon decision making in large language models.

title: G-RRM: Guiding Symbolic Solvers with Recurrent Reasoning Models
headline: Guiding Symbolic Solvers Using Recurrent Reasoning Models for Constraint Satisfaction
tldr: We introduce G-RRM, a neuro-symbolic framework that combines symbol-equivariant recurrent reasoning models with symbolic solvers to improve extrapolation on larger constraint satisfaction problems. SE-RRMs generate full solution proposals, guiding the solver and enhancing performance beyond traditional neural approaches.
significance: G-RRM demonstrates that integrating recurrent reasoning models with symbolic solvers yields scalable, interpretable solutions for complex constraint satisfaction tasks.

title: Cryogenic RF characterization of the MAGO cavity for high-frequency gravitational-wave detection
headline: Cryogenic RF Tests of Novel Niobium SRF Cavity for High-Frequency Gravitational Wave Detection
tldr: We performed cryogenic RF characterization on a prototype niobium SRF cavity with a unique geometry that enhances mode separation. The results demonstrate the cavity’s suitability as a resonant sensor for detecting gravitational waves in the kHz–MHz band.
significance: This work establishes a viable superconducting cavity design for high-frequency gravitational-wave detectors, potentially expanding the observable frequency range.

title: QuadRocket: An Aerial Robotic Testbed for Adaptive Thrust-Vector Control of Rocket-Like Vehicles
headline: QuadRocket: Low-Cost Quadrotor-Based Rocket Prototype Testing Adaptive Thrust-Vector Control for Launch Vehicle Systems
tldr: This paper introduces QuadRocket, a low-cost quadrotor-mounted rocket prototype that serves as a flying inverted pendulum for validating thrust-vector control strategies. The platform enables real-time, high-fidelity experiments on launch vehicle dynamics with reduced risk and cost.
significance: QuadRocket provides a scalable, low-risk testbed that accelerates the development of advanced launch vehicle control systems.

title: Probabilistic Memory for Trustworthy Edge Intelligence
headline: Probabilistic Memory Enhances Edge AI Reliability and Efficiency
tldr: This work presents probabilistic memory (p-MEM), a hardware/software architecture that bridges the data throughput gap between Gaussian random number generation and probabilistic computation, enabling trustworthy edge intelligence with lower instruction overhead. By integrating p-MEM, edge devices can quantify uncertainty, improve robustness, and protect privacy while maintaining real‑time performance.
significance: p-MEM unlocks scalable probabilistic inference on resource‑constrained edge devices, paving the way for reliable, privacy‑preserving AI in real‑time applications.

title: Will Scaling Improve Social Simulation with LLMs?
headline: Can Scaling LLMs Close the Gap in Social Simulation Fidelity?
tldr: This study applies scaling laws to assess whether larger language models can improve the realism of social simulations. Findings suggest that while size boosts general performance, it does not fully resolve fidelity gaps, indicating a need for targeted research.
significance: It highlights that scaling alone may not suffice for high-fidelity social simulations, guiding future research priorities.

title: Topological Control of Quantum Chaos Diagnostics: OTOCs, Spectral Statistics, and Information Scrambling in Ising Model
headline: Graph Topology Accelerates Quantum Chaos and Information Scrambling in Ising Models
tldr: We study how different network topologies affect the transition from integrability to chaos in Ising spin systems. Long-range couplings and heterogeneous degree distributions significantly speed up quantum information propagation, as revealed by OTOCs, spectral statistics, and scrambling metrics.
significance: These findings provide a graph-theoretic framework for designing quantum systems with tailored scrambling dynamics, useful for quantum information processing and simulation.

title: Quantum mutual information as a robust probe of integrability in open quantum systems
headline: Quantum Mutual Information Reveals Integrability in Open Quantum Systems Using Haar-Averaged Correlations
tldr: We introduce a Haar-averaged sum of total correlations (aSTC) as an information-theoretic probe for integrability in open quantum systems. The method distinguishes integrable from chaotic dynamics and offers a robust, scalable diagnostic.
significance: This framework provides a scalable, experimentally accessible metric to detect quantum chaos, bridging theory and measurement in open systems.

title: OrbitQuant: Data-Agnostic Quantization for Image and Video Diffusion Transformers
headline: OrbitQuant: Data‑Agnostic Quantization for Faster Diffusion Transformers Across Modalities and Use‑Cases
tldr: OrbitQuant introduces a data‑agnostic post‑training quantization method that eliminates the need for per‑checkpoint calibration in diffusion transformers. This approach enables real‑time inference across image and video generation tasks while preserving model accuracy.
significance: It dramatically reduces inference cost for diffusion transformers, making them more accessible for real‑time applications.

title: Neuron-Aware Data Selection for Annotation-Free LLM Self-Distillation
headline: Neuron-Aware Data Selection Boosts Annotation-Free Self-Distillation for Specialized LLM Domains
tldr: This study introduces a neuron-aware data selection strategy that enhances annotation-free self-distillation for large language models. By leveraging the model’s own predictions as supervisory signals and aggregating them with contextual cues, the approach improves performance in specialized domains without costly expert labeling.
significance: It demonstrates a scalable path to refine LLMs in niche fields without human annotation, potentially accelerating deployment in resource-constrained settings.

title: Language Models as Measurement Apparatus for Culture
headline: Language Models as Cultural Measurement Tools: A Material-Discursive Perspective
tldr: This paper argues that language models used to quantify culture are not passive recorders but active participants in shaping cultural reality. By applying Karen Barad’s concept of agential realism, it shows how models, data, annotation, and evaluation co-constitute the phenomena they aim to measure.
significance: It reframes NLP studies of culture as transformative practices that influence the very cultural constructs they analyze.

title: Nonholonomic Source Seeking by Torque Tuning: Local and Semi-Global Feedbacks
headline: Torque Tuning Enables Nonholonomic Vehicle to Seek Unknown Sources in Real Time
tldr: This study presents a feedback control strategy that tunes torque to guide a nonholonomic vehicle with a laterally displaced sensor toward an unknown source while maintaining constant forward speed. The approach achieves local and semi-global stability without requiring position measurements, enabling real-time source‑seeking in uncertain dynamics.
significance: It offers a robust, measurement‑free method for autonomous source tracking in environments with unknown vehicle dynamics.

title: A Quantum-Walk Representation of Color-Ordered MHV Scattering Amplitudes
headline: Quantum Walks on Permutation Trees Model Color-Ordered MHV Scattering Amplitudes
tldr: We propose a graph-theoretic framework using coined quantum walks on permutation trees to represent color-ordered MHV scattering amplitudes. Each path corresponds to a distinct gluon ordering, with transition amplitudes reflecting the Parke–Taylor spinor-product structure.
significance: This approach offers a novel combinatorial perspective that could streamline analytic calculations and inspire new computational algorithms for scattering amplitudes.

title: When Do LLM Personas Support Visualization Design? A Cross-Model Study of Color Assignment and Chart Choice
headline: LLM Personas and Visualization Design: When Do They Truly Reflect User Preferences?
tldr: This study investigates whether persona-conditioned outputs from large language models reliably capture user preferences in visualization tasks. Across color assignment and chart choice, results suggest that persona effects vary by model and task framing, highlighting the need for careful evaluation.
significance: It clarifies the reliability of LLM personas for early-stage visualization design, guiding practitioners on when to trust persona-driven recommendations.

title: Adoption and Ecosystem Health: A Longitudinal Analysis of Open-Source Multi-Agent Frameworks
headline: Tracking the Rise of Open‑Source Multi‑Agent Frameworks: 2022‑2026 Adoption Trends
tldr: From ChatGPT’s debut, 15 major agent frameworks have amassed over 800k stars and nearly a million users. This study maps their growth, pull‑request activity, and commit patterns to reveal which frameworks sustain healthy ecosystems.
significance: Understanding which open‑source agent frameworks thrive helps engineering teams choose reliable, community‑supported tools.

title: AgentsCAD: Automated Design for Manufacturing of FDM Parts via Multi-Agent LLM Reasoning and Geometric Feature Recognition
headline: AgentsCAD: Multi-Agent LLM System Enhancing Automated FDM Part Design for Manufacturing
tldr: AgentsCAD uses a multi-agent large language model framework to automatically modify CAD geometry for FDM printing, addressing overhangs and structural weaknesses. By integrating geometric feature recognition, the system generates printable designs without manual intervention, streamlining the DFAM workflow.
significance: This approach democratizes DFAM by enabling non-experts to produce print-ready designs, reducing time-to-market and material waste.

title: VT-WAM: Visual-Tactile World Action Model for Contact-Rich Manipulation
headline: VT-WAM: Modeling Tactile Dynamics for Real-Time Contact-Rich Manipulation
tldr: VT-WAM introduces a visual-tactile action model that explicitly captures tactile deformation dynamics during contact-rich manipulation, enabling policies to react to sparse tactile cues. The model demonstrates improved performance in tasks requiring precise force control and slip detection, highlighting the importance of integrating tactile dynamics into action generation.
significance: By modeling tactile deformation dynamics, VT-WAM bridges the gap between visual perception and tactile feedback, advancing the reliability of robotic manipulation in complex contact scenarios.

title: Embodied.cpp: A Portable Inference Runtime of Embodied AI Models on Heterogeneous Robots
headline: Embodied.cpp: Portable Runtime for Embodied AI on Heterogeneous Robots and Edge Devices
tldr: Embodied.cpp provides a lightweight, cross‑platform inference runtime that unifies vision‑language‑action and world‑action models, enabling seamless deployment on diverse robotic hardware. It eliminates fragmented Python stacks and backend dependencies, offering real‑time inference without the overhead of traditional request‑response servers.
significance: This runtime bridges the gap between advanced embodied AI research and practical robotic deployment, accelerating real‑world applications across heterogeneous edge devices.

title: EAGLE-360: Embodied Active Global-to-Local Exploration in 360$^\circ$
headline: EAGLE-360: Overcoming Panoramic Distortion in Active Visual Search with MLLMs
tldr: EAGLE-360 introduces a framework for embodied active exploration in 360° panoramic scenes, addressing the polar distortion and cylindrical topology challenges that limit standard multimodal LLMs. By integrating specialized geometric modeling and real-time navigation, it enables more accurate object detection and spatial reasoning in panoramic environments.
significance: This work bridges the gap between large language models and panoramic perception, paving the way for robust autonomous agents in VR, robotics, and surveillance.

title: Mixing times of spin systems on dynamical percolation
headline: Analyzing Mixing Times for Glauber Dynamics on Dynamical Percolation Networks
tldr: We investigate how quickly Glauber dynamics on a torus with randomly evolving edges reach equilibrium. The study reveals that the mixing time scales with the percolation parameter and dimension, providing new bounds for stochastic spin systems.
significance: These results bridge percolation theory and Markov chain mixing, offering insights into the convergence behavior of spatially evolving stochastic processes.

title: Audio-Based Understanding of Audiobook Narration Appeal
headline: Analyzing Audiobook Narration: Audio Features Drive Listener Appeal Across Genres
tldr: This study extracts vocal and acoustic features from LibriVox audiobooks using pre-trained audio models to quantify how narration quality influences listener engagement. Results show that tone, pace, and loudness significantly affect appeal, with variations across genre and audience.
significance: Understanding narration appeal can guide audiobook producers to tailor vocal delivery for maximum audience engagement.

title: Learning Agile Intruder Interception using Differentiable Quadrotor Dynamics
headline: Learning Agile Intruder Interception with Differentiable Quadrotor Dynamics Using Passive Monocular Vision
tldr: This paper introduces a reinforcement learning framework that learns to intercept an intruder using only the 3‑D direction vector to the target and the interceptor’s state, eliminating the need for explicit relative position or distance measurements. By leveraging differentiable quadrotor dynamics and passive monocular vision, the method achieves real‑time, agile interception in scenarios where traditional sensors are unavailable.
significance: It enables autonomous drones to pursue and intercept moving targets in realistic vision‑limited environments, broadening the applicability of UAV interception systems.

title: Interpretation-Oriented Cloud Removal via Observation-Anchored Residual Flow with Geo-Contextual Alignment
headline: New Cloud Removal Method Enhances Downstream Remote Sensing Interpretation Accuracy
tldr: This work introduces an observation-anchored residual flow for cloud removal that aligns geo-contextual information, ensuring that the cleaned imagery preserves semantic integrity for tasks like segmentation and change detection. Unlike prior methods that focus on visual realism, the proposed approach prioritizes downstream analytical performance, reducing semantic drift.
significance: By aligning cloud removal with downstream interpretation needs, the method improves the reliability of remote sensing analytics, enabling more accurate environmental monitoring and decision-making.

title: TestEvo-Bench: An Executable and Live Benchmark for Test and Code Co-Evolution
headline: TestEvo-Bench: A Live Benchmark for Executable Test and Code Co‑Evolution
tldr: TestEvo-Bench provides an executable, live benchmark that ties tests directly to code changes, ensuring tests are semantically linked and runnable. This approach overcomes limitations of prior benchmarks that rely on static metadata and isolate tests from code evolution.
significance: By validating executable tests in real time, TestEvo-Bench enables more accurate assessment of test generation tools and promotes reliable software maintenance.

title: Bifurcations of the Clifford Torus as Willmore Surfaces in Berger Spheres
headline: Willmore Torus Bifurcations in Berger Spheres
tldr: The Clifford torus remains a Willmore critical point for all Berger sphere parameters, and Morse index analysis reveals bifurcations yielding new symmetric tori.
significance: It uncovers new families of Willmore surfaces via bifurcation theory.

title: Human Capital, Not Model Benchmarks, Predicts Hybrid Intelligence in Forecasting
headline: Human Capital, Not Model Benchmarks, Drives Hybrid Forecasting Success
tldr: A study using Polymarket shows that the benefit of pairing humans with AI in forecasting depends on specific human skills rather than model quality. Individual forecasters’ hybrid performance is trimodal, highlighting the importance of targeted human capital.
significance: The findings suggest that investing in human expertise is more critical than improving AI models for effective hybrid forecasting.

title: Learning to Move Before Learning to Do: Task-Agnostic pretraining for VLAs
headline: Separating Movement from Meaning: Task‑Agnostic Pretraining for Vision‑Language‑Action Models at Scale
tldr: The paper identifies that VLA models suffer from a lack of expert demonstrations, which conflate learning to move with learning what to do. It proposes a task‑agnostic pretraining approach that first learns physical motion before aligning semantics, improving data efficiency and performance.
significance: By decoupling motion and semantic learning, the method reduces dependence on costly demonstrations, accelerating VLA model development.

title: A Probabilistic Sign Rule for Quotients of Positive Series and Integral Transforms
headline: Probabilistic Sign Rule for Quotients of Positive Series and Integral Transforms
tldr: This paper introduces a probabilistic sign rule that links the sign of quotients of functions represented by positive series or integrals to probability laws derived from normalized summands or integrands. By expressing parameter log-derivatives as moments of kernels, the authors provide a novel framework for analyzing complex function quotients.
significance: It offers a powerful tool for mathematical analysis, enabling new insights into the behavior of function quotients across various applications.

title: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay as LLM Harness for Long-Context Reasoning
headline: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay Enhances Long-Context Reasoning for Real-World LLM Applications
tldr: ReContext introduces a recursive evidence replay mechanism that allows LLMs to revisit and leverage relevant information within long inputs, improving reasoning accuracy. The approach demonstrates significant gains on benchmark tasks, bridging the gap between context size and effective utilization.
significance: By enabling LLMs to systematically reuse evidence, ReContext paves the way for more reliable reasoning in data-intensive applications.

title: Beyond Adam: SOAP and Muon for Faster, Label-Efficient Training of Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
headline: Optimizing ML Interatomic Potentials: Beyond Adam with SOAP and Muon
tldr: Researchers compare new optimizers to Adam for training machine learning interatomic potentials, achieving faster convergence and reduced label requirements. The study demonstrates that SOAP and Muon frameworks can significantly improve training efficiency and accuracy.
significance: This work highlights the importance of optimizer selection in MLIP training, offering a path to more efficient and scalable simulations.

title: Seek to Segment: Active Perception for Panoramic Referring Segmentation
headline: Active Panoramic Referring Segmentation: Enabling Embodied AI to Navigate 360° Environments
tldr: Traditional referring segmentation models process static images, limiting their use in embodied AI. Our new Active Panoramic Referring Segmentation task allows agents to actively adjust viewpoints in continuous 360° scenes to segment referred objects.
significance: This work bridges the gap between passive vision models and active perception required for real-world embodied agents.

title: Controllable Sim Agents with Behavior Latents
headline: Controllable Simulated Agents Using Behavior Latents for Realistic Traffic Modeling
tldr: We propose Controllable Neural Variational Agents (CNeVA) that learn to infer per-agent behavior latent variables from logged traffic data, enabling steering along interpretable axes. This controllability allows engineers to isolate variables, reproduce edge cases, and test autonomous systems safely without real-world deployment.
significance: CNeVA bridges the gap between realistic simulation fidelity and controllable scenario generation, accelerating safe autonomous vehicle testing.

title: Towards Robustness against Typographic Attack with Training-free Concept Localization
headline: Enhancing CLIP Robustness: Training-Free Localization Against Typographic Attacks
tldr: This study introduces a training-free concept localization technique to mitigate typographic attacks that mislead CLIP-based vision encoders. By isolating visual concepts from textual noise, the method improves robustness without additional training.
significance: It demonstrates a lightweight, data-free defense that preserves CLIP's performance while protecting against adversarial text artifacts.

title: Cut-off Jastrow Factors and Spectral Barron Regularity of Coulombic Electronic Wave Functions
headline: Spectral Barron Regularity of Coulombic Wave Functions via Cut-off Jastrow Extraction
tldr: We analyze the spectral Barron regularity of Coulombic electronic eigenfunctions after removing a cut-off Jastrow factor. The study extends previous results on Coulomb Hamiltonians and provides new insights into wave function smoothness.
significance: This work deepens our understanding of electronic wave function regularity, which is crucial for accurate quantum chemical computations.

title: Visually Grounded Self-Reflection for Vision-Language Models via Reinforcement Learning
headline: Grounding Self-Reflection in Vision‑Language Models for Accurate Visual Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
tldr: The paper introduces a reinforcement learning framework that trains vision‑language models to revisit and correct their textual reasoning steps while ensuring alignment with visual evidence. Experiments show that visually grounded self‑reflection improves accuracy on multimodal benchmarks compared to standard chain‑of‑thought methods.
significance: By integrating visual grounding into self‑reflection, the work bridges a key gap in multimodal reasoning, enabling LVLMs to produce more reliable, evidence‑based conclusions.

title: The Debris Disk Host $β$ Piscis Austrinus is a Rapidly Rotating Star Seen Nearly Pole-On
headline: Rapidly Rotating Star β Piscis Austrinus Revealed as Pole-On via High-Resolution Spectroscopy
tldr: Using the fastrot-spec code, we modeled HARPS spectra of β PsA to determine its inclination, confirming it is a rapidly rotating star seen nearly pole-on. This provides new insights into its photospheric properties and rotational dynamics.
significance: The study refines our understanding of stellar rotation effects on spectral line shapes, aiding accurate characterization of rapidly rotating stars.

title: GeoMix: Descriptor-Free Visual Localization via Global Context and Multi-Detector Training
headline: GeoMix Enhances Descriptor-Free Visual Localization with Global Context and Multi-Detector Training
tldr: GeoMix introduces a descriptor-free localization pipeline that leverages global context and multi-detector training to improve geometric discriminability. The approach reduces storage, preserves privacy, and narrows the performance gap with descriptor-based methods.
significance: By boosting accuracy while eliminating descriptor storage, GeoMix paves the way for scalable, privacy-preserving visual localization in large-scale mapping.
